specification:
Type: MicroSim **sim-id:** lawn-carbon-comparison
**Library:** Chart.js
**Status:** Specified A stacked bar chart comparing annual CO₂-equivalent emissions from turf grass vs. moss lawn maintenance: **Turf Grass Bar (stacked segments):** - Mowing (gasoline): 40 kg CO₂e — dark red - Fertilizer production: 80 kg CO₂e — orange - N₂O from fertilizer: 150 kg CO₂e — yellow - Irrigation pumping: 30 kg CO₂e — blue - Other equipment: 20 kg CO₂e — gray - Total: ~320 kg CO₂e (conservative estimate for 200 m²) **Moss Lawn Bar:** - Maintenance: 0 kg CO₂e - Carbon sequestration offset: -5 to -15 kg CO₂e (shown as green bar below zero) **Controls:** - Toggle to switch between "per 200 m²" and "per acre" scales - Hover tooltips showing exact values and explanations Canvas: responsive width, 400px height Color scheme: warm colors for emissions, green for sequestration **Learning objective:** (L4 — Analyze) Students can compare the carbon footprint of turf grass and moss lawns and identify the major sources of lawn-related emissions. Implementation: Chart.js stacked bar chart with tooltip annotations
